title: Hollywood is bending the knee to OpenAI
slides: Why is Hollywood suddenly bowing to OpenAI?
Studios are licensing footage for AI training.
They once sued over the same technology.
Your next film script may co-star an AI.
Test usingSora for your next storyboard now.
thread_text:
1. Why surrender now?

Disney just sold hours of unseen content to OpenAI for training. The same studios that sued last year.

2. They sued first.

Last fall, studios filed injunctions against AI scraping. Now they're signing checks for the same data.

3. Your next co-writer.

Sora can generate storyboards from text prompts. Studios are testing scripts with AI in the writer's room today.

4. The silent shift.

Licensing deals let studios control the training data. The real fight isn't over art—it's over who owns the past.

5. Test Sora now.

Take a script page you wrote this week and run it through Sora. If the output saves you one board revision, keep going.
video_script: Hollywood sued OpenAI last year. Now they pay OpenAI.

script: Hollywood sued OpenAI last year. Now they pay OpenAI.
Disney licensed unseen footage for AI training.
Your next storyboard can be generated by Sora.
Studios control data, not creativity anymore.
Test Sora on your script this afternoon.

instagram.caption:
Studios that sued OpenAI for scraping their content last year are now licensing them hours of unseen footage for training. 🎬

Disney just signed a deal that hands over proprietary material to the same company they tried to block in court. The shift isn't about art — it's about who gets paid for their back catalog.

If you write or storyboard for a living, your next project might be reviewed by a model trained directly on the films you grew up on. ✍️

When was the last time you checked whether your portfolio is already feeding a competitor's model?
